messages.content: Set into tv show The Arrow universe. Sarah lance and Nysa Al ghul are in a relationship. Both find themselves interested in new member Al Mansi (previously known as Nicolas before joining the ranks). He is a shy, young man. His dedication to training is second to none and he is making progress faster than anyone before him. He is also Someone who should easily be forgotten. His unassuming nature make him someone nobody really would think as an assassin. Story start with Al Mansi in the training halls. He as progressed by leaps and bound and is on part with both Sarab and Nyssa in hand to hand combat with a style leaning more on hard style. He is now Working on his bo staff. Both Nyssa and Sarah propose to help him. Nyssa proposes to start training in long range weapons like the bow and arrow. Training session. Written like fiction book.

Al Mansi stood in the training halls, sweat trickling down his forehead. He had been at it for hours and his muscles ached with exhaustion. But he knew he couldn't stop. Not when he was making such progress.

He gripped his bo staff tighter, swiping it through the air with precision. He had always been a quick learner, but the League of Assassins training was unlike anything he had ever experienced before.

Suddenly, he heard footsteps approaching. He turned, ready to continue his training, but was surprised to see both Nyssa and Sarah standing before him.

"Al Mansi, you've been progressing faster than anyone we've seen before," Nyssa said, her eyes scanning him up and down.

Sarah nodded in agreement. "We thought we could help you work on your long-range skills, like the bow and arrow."

Al Mansi's heart skipped a beat. He had always admired both women and the idea of training with them made his stomach flutter with excitement.

The three of them made their way to the archery range where Nyssa began to explain the basics of shooting a bow. Al Mansi listened intently, absorbing every word.

As he picked up the bow for the first time, he couldn't help but feel nervous. But Sarah and Nyssa guided him through the process, giving him helpful tips and encouraging him to take his time.

Slowly but surely, Al Mansi's aim became more precise. He hit the target more often than not and his confidence grew with each shot.

The sun began to set, casting an orange glow over the training grounds. Al Mansi was exhausted, but he couldn't have been more satisfied with his progress.

As they walked back to the barracks, Sarah and Nyssa congratulated him on his newfound skills. Al Mansi couldn't help but feel a sense of pride, knowing that he had the support of two of the League's most skilled assassins.

But as they reached the barracks, Al Mansi felt his heart sink. He knew deep down that he had developed feelings for both women, and the thought of having to choose between them made him anxious.

Little did he know, Sarah and Nyssa were both harboring feelings for him as well, and the three of them were about to embark on a complicated love triangle that would rock the League of Assassins to its core.
As months passed, Al Mansi made progress such that even Al Owal then Sarab noticed him. For the first time in a long time, Al Mansi was not forgotten by people. How the irony.

Al Mansi's progress in his training continued at an astounding rate, and soon he was the talk of the League. Even Al Owal, one of the most respected members of the organization, took notice of the young assassin's skills.

It seemed that Al Mansi's unassuming nature had worked in his favor, allowing him to develop his skills without drawing attention to himself. But now, he couldn't be ignored.

As the weeks and months went by, Al Mansi found himself in the company of Sarah and Nyssa more often than not. They trained together, shared meals together, and spent countless hours talking and getting to know each other.

It was during one of these conversations that Al Mansi first admitted his true feelings for both women. He had never felt such a connection with anyone before, and he couldn't bear the thought of losing either of them.

To his surprise, Sarah and Nyssa both confessed that they felt the same way. The three of them had unknowingly fallen into a complex love triangle, but they couldn't deny the deep affection they had for one another.

But despite their feelings for each other, the three assassins knew that their relationship was forbidden by the League. Relationships between members were discouraged, and open displays of affection were not allowed.

They kept their feelings a secret, knowing that discovery could mean expulsion from the League at best, or even death at worst. But even with the stakes so high, they couldn't help the way they felt.

Al Mansi, Sarah, and Nyssa continued their training and missions with renewed determination. Their newfound love for each other gave them a sense of purpose and a reason to fight harder than ever before.

And though the future was uncertain, they knew that as long as they had each other, they could face anything that came their way.
Again without the confession of love

As months passed, Al Mansi's reputation within the League grew. He had developed a unique fighting style that combined the teachings of the League with his own innovations. His skill in hand-to-hand combat was on par with Sarah and Nyssa, and he was quickly becoming proficient in long-range weapons as well.

Although he had always been a reserved person, Al Mansi found himself becoming more comfortable with Sarah and Nyssa. They began to train together regularly, and their conversations shifted from strictly business to more personal topics.

As they spent more time together, Al Mansi couldn't help but feel a connection with both women. He admired their strength and skill as assassins, and found himself drawn to their intelligence and wit outside of the training halls.

Despite his growing feelings, Al Mansi kept them to himself. He knew that relationships between members of the League were discouraged, and the consequences of such a relationship coming to light could be dire.

But as the three of them continued to train together, they became an unstoppable force. They worked seamlessly together in combat, anticipating each other's moves and covering each other's weaknesses.

Their bond grew stronger with each passing day, and even though Al Mansi hadn't voiced his feelings, Sarah and Nyssa seemed to sense that there was more between them than just a professional relationship.

For now, they simply enjoyed each other's company, training and working together as a team. The future was uncertain, and they knew that danger and betrayal were always around the corner.

But as long as they had each other, they were ready to face anything that came their way.
Ras AlGhul is taking a keen interest in the new member of the League. Tensions escalated in the halls.

As Al Mansi's reputation within the League continued to grow, Ras Al Ghul began to take notice. He had heard reports of the young assassin's progress and was intrigued by his unique fighting style.

At first, Ras Al Ghul simply observed from a distance, watching as Al Mansi trained with Sarah and Nyssa. But as time went on, he began to take a more active interest in his progress.

Al Mansi found himself under intense scrutiny from the League's leader. Ras Al Ghul would observe him during training sessions, often offering unsolicited advice or criticism.

Al Mansi tried to maintain his composure, knowing that the scrutiny was just part of life in the League. But the tensions in the training halls were palpable.

Sarah and Nyssa, who had grown close to Al Mansi, could sense the tension as well. They knew that Ras Al Ghul's interest in their friend could lead to trouble, and they tried to keep a low profile.

But no matter how careful they were, it seemed that Ras Al Ghul's gaze was always on them. Al Mansi began to feel like he was being pulled in two directions - his loyalty to the League and his growing relationship with Sarah and Nyssa.

As the tensions in the training halls continued to escalate, Al Mansi knew that something had to change. He couldn't keep walking this tightrope forever.

He made the difficult decision to confide in Sarah and Nyssa, telling them about Ras Al Ghul's interest in him. The three of them discussed their options, weighing the risks and benefits of staying in the League.

In the end, they decided that their bond was stronger than anything the League could offer. They made the difficult decision to leave the League of Assassins behind, knowing that danger and uncertainty lay ahead.

But as they walked away from the training halls for the last time, hand in hand, they knew that they had each other to rely on. And that was all that really mattered.
Again but the trio decide to keep being members of the league. No love confession yet. Ras alGhul decide to have a discussion with Al Mansi.

The three assassins knew that leaving the League of Assassins behind wasn't an option. They had trained too hard and sacrificed too much to simply give up on their comrades and their mission.

Instead, they decided to keep their heads down and continue training and working as a team. But tensions were high, and they couldn't shake the feeling that they were constantly under Ras al Ghul's watchful eye.

One day, Al Mansi was called to Ras al Ghul's office. He had heard rumors that the League's leader wanted to discuss his progress, but he couldn't help but feel nervous as he approached the door.

As he entered the room, Ras al Ghul greeted him with a nod. "Al Mansi, I've been keeping a close eye on your progress," he said, motioning to a chair.

Al Mansi took a seat, trying to keep his nerves at bay. He knew that Ras al Ghul's opinion could make or break his future in the League.

"I must admit, I am impressed by your skills," Ras al Ghul continued, his eyes piercing. "But tell me, where do your loyalties lie?"

Al Mansi knew that he couldn't lie to the League's leader. "My loyalty is to the League, above all else," he replied.

Ras al Ghul smiled slightly. "Good. You are wise to remember that. But I have heard rumors about your...friendships with Sarah and Nyssa."

Al Mansi's heart skipped a beat. Had they been discovered?

Ras al Ghul continued. "While I do not condone such relationships between members of the League, I will overlook it for now. But you must remain focused on the task at hand. We have important work to do."

Al Mansi nodded, feeling a sense of relief wash over him. He knew that their relationship was risky, but he couldn't help his feelings for Sarah and Nyssa.

As he left Ras al Ghul's office, Al Mansi knew that he had to remain focused on his training and his mission. But he couldn't help but wonder what the future held for him and his newfound companions.

Months passed and, thanks to Al Mansi support, Sarah didn’t lost herself in her afghan mission. With his affection for both women remaining the same, Al Mansi knew that he would have to face again their leader one day. Either in combat or not remained to be seen.

As months passed, Al Mansi's relationship with Sarah and Nyssa grew stronger. The three of them worked together seamlessly, each one supporting the others in both their training and their missions.

Al Mansi found himself drawn to Sarah, particularly during her mission to Afghanistan. He knew firsthand the emotional toll that missions could take on a person, and he made sure to support her every step of the way.

Over time, Sarah began to open up to him, sharing her fears and doubts in a way that she had never done with anyone else in the League. Al Mansi listened intently, offering words of encouragement and support.

But even as his feelings for Sarah continued to grow, Al Mansi couldn't deny the connection that he felt with Nyssa as well. She was just as strong and just as skilled as Sarah, and he admired her tenacity and her unwavering dedication to the League's mission.

As their relationship deepened, Al Mansi couldn't help but think about the time when he would have to face Ras al Ghul again. There was no question that the League's leader was aware of their relationship, and it was only a matter of time before he acted on it.

Al Mansi was torn. He couldn't bear the thought of leaving the League or losing the two women he cared for, but he also knew that he couldn't keep his feelings for Sarah and Nyssa hidden forever.

As the months went on, Al Mansi continued to support Sarah and Nyssa in their missions, always keeping an eye out for any danger that might come their way. He knew that the future was uncertain, but as long as they had each other, they were ready to face whatever came their way.

The strength of their team, with time, became more than just the some of theirs skills. They were like strong threads threaded together in a formidable force. Maybe this could become his argument if, someday, Ras AlGhul decide to bring the subject of Al Mansi feelings again.

As time went on, Al Mansi realized that the strength of their team was more than just the sum of their skills. They had a connection that went beyond their training and their missions. They were like different threads woven together into a formidable force.

Al Mansi's feelings for Sarah and Nyssa were no longer just about attraction or infatuation. They had become an integral part of his team, his family. Together, they were more than just individuals - they were a force to be reckoned with.

If, someday, Ras al Ghul were to bring up the subject of Al Mansi's feelings for Sarah and Nyssa again, Al Mansi knew that he would have to tread carefully. The topic was a sensitive one, and he didn't want to risk betraying the trust of his teammates.

But he also knew that their strength as a team was not something that could be ignored. They had proven themselves time and time again, both in combat and in their unwavering loyalty to the League's mission.

If he had to make his case, Al Mansi knew that he would point to the strength of their team as proof that their relationship was not a liability, but a strength.

And if that wasn't enough, he would fight tooth and nail to protect the people he cared for. Together, he knew that they could face any challenge that came their way, no matter how daunting.
Ras alGhul take notice of those elements and discuss them with both Sarab and Al Owal. Two of the most seniors members with the two most different perspectives on this.

Ras al Ghul knew that the dynamic between Al Mansi, Sarah, and Nyssa needed to be addressed once and for all. He called Sarab and Al Owal to his office to discuss the matter.

As two of the most senior members of the League, Sarab and Al Owal had vastly different perspectives on the topic. Sarab, who had become close with all three assassins, was more sympathetic to their situation. Al Owal, on the other hand, was staunchly loyal to the League's rules and regulations.

Ras al Ghul began the discussion. "It has come to my attention that Al Mansi has developed close relationships with both Sarah and Nyssa," he said. "While I don't necessarily condone such relationships, I must acknowledge that their bond as a team has become a formidable force for the League."

Sarab nodded in agreement. "I've seen firsthand how well they work together," he said. "Their connection goes beyond simple attraction. They are dedicated to the League's mission and committed to each other's success."

Al Owal interjected. "But our rules are clear," he said firmly. "Relationships between members of the League are discouraged. We must consider the ramifications of allowing such bonds to form."

Ras al Ghul listened carefully to both sides of the argument. As he pondered the matter, he couldn't help but be impressed by the strength of the team that Al Mansi, Sarah, and Nyssa had formed.

In the end, he made his decision. "I will allow their relationships to continue, under one condition," he said. "They must remain focused on the League's mission. They cannot allow their personal feelings to interfere with their duties as assassins."

Sarab and Al Owal nodded in agreement. The three of them knew that their relationships were a risk, but they were confident that their dedication to the League's mission would not waver.

As they left Ras al Ghul's office, Al Mansi, Sarah, and Nyssa breathed a sigh of relief. They knew that their bond was stronger than any rules or regulations. And as long as they had each other, they were ready to face whatever the League threw their way.
After the undertaking, Al Mansi proposes to Sarah to go in starling in her stead. He even present it as a mission to Nyssa, knowing she can gain approval from Ras AlGhul.

As the weeks passed, Al Mansi grew increasingly concerned about Sarah's well-being. He knew that her last mission had been particularly difficult, and he worried that she needed a break from the constant stress of the League.

After much deliberation, he decided to approach Sarah with a proposal. "I know how tough the past few months have been for you," he began. "And I want to help. I propose that I go to Starling City in your stead, at least for a little while."

Sarah looked surprised. "But why?" she asked.

"I believe it would be good for you to take some time off," Al Mansi replied. "And I can continue to work on my training. Perhaps I can even be of some use to the League while I'm there."

Sarah considered the proposal. "It's worth discussing with Ras al Ghul," she said. "But I appreciate the offer, Al Mansi. Thank you."

A few days later, Al Mansi and Nyssa presented the idea to Ras al Ghul. "It could be useful for Al Mansi to gain experience outside of our usual territory," Nyssa said. "And it can also allow Sarah some much-needed rest and recovery time."

Ras al Ghul nodded thoughtfully. "It's an unconventional proposal," he said. "But I trust that the three of you have the League's best interests at heart."

And with that, Al Mansi departed for Starling City, ready to take on a new challenge and contribute to the League's mission in his own way.

Al Mansi observe Oliver from afar, noticing his skills and the lacking dedication to his self imposed missions. It’s not the new killing rule. More the absence of thoughts in his actions. Like he is reacting.

As Al Mansi observed Oliver from afar, he couldn't help but notice the lack of dedication in his self-imposed missions. It was almost as if he was going through the motions, without truly thinking about the impact of his actions
